text: Ugh, another meeting about spreadsheets. Can we move to emojis already? #SpreadsheetSnoozefest
label: BORED

text: Watching paint dry is starting to sound more exciting than this lecture. ‍♀️ #SomeoneWakeMeUp

text: Small talk at a party is like watching grass grow.  #GiveMeAGoodBook

text: Scrolling through endless scrolling news updates. Is anything even happening anymore?  #BigYawn

text: Another movie night with the same rom-com plot. Can't Hollywood come up with something new?   #BeenThereSeenThat
label: BORED

text: Stuck in traffic. Guess I'll just become one with my phone for the next hour.   #TrafficBoredom ️

text: Folding laundry is the chore equivalent of watching paint dry. Can robots do this yet? #LaundryLand

text: Been staring at this computer screen for hours. My brain feels like mush.  #NeedAMentalBreak

text: Instructions for furniture assembly? More like instructions for falling asleep.   #IKEAInsomnia

text: Waiting for the dentist. Maybe I should've just stuck with the toothache.  #DentalDread

text: Watching reality TV.  Is this what peak entertainment looks like?  #RealityTVBoredom

text: Stuck on hold with customer service. Hold music is doing a terrible job of holding my attention.  #HoldMusicHell

text: Another day, another endless scroll through social media. Feeling  less informed and more bored.   #SocialMediaSnooze

text: Listening to someone drone on about their vacation photos. Wish I could teleport to a beach right now. ️ #VacationBoredom  ✈️

text: Trying to find something interesting to watch. Everything just seems like a rehash of something else.   #ContentDrought

text: Stuck in a meeting that could have been an email. My patience is wearing thinner than this chair.  🪑 #MeetingMarathon

text: Another day of endless errands. Is adulting just a never-ending to-do list?   #ErrandsEnnui

text: Reading a textbook that could put a sloth to sleep.   #TextbookTedium

text: Cleaning the house. Wish dirt and dust would just clean themselves already.   #CleaningConundrum
